---
title: "l2_normalize"
generated: true
description: "Project `x` onto the unit $L_2$ sphere along `axis`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.normalize`](../nitrix-numerics-normalize.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:l2_normalize -->
```python
l2_normalize(x: Float[Array, '...'], *, axis: _AxisArg = -1, eps: float = 1e-12) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:l2_normalize -->
Project `x` onto the unit $L_2$ sphere along `axis`.

The $p = 2$ specialisation of [`lp_normalize`](../nitrix-numerics-normalize/lp_normalize.qmd),
computed directly from the sum of squares (avoiding the general
$|x|^p$ power and its root) and clamping the denominator
at `eps` (the `torch.nn.functional.normalize` convention
$x / \max(\|x\|_2, \epsilon)$).  Once rows are unit-norm
their inner product *is* the cosine of the angle between them,
so this is the projection that turns a dot product into a
cosine similarity / angular distance.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:l2_normalize -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` | — | Input tensor. |
| `axis` | `_AxisArg` | — | `-1` | Axis (or axes) over which the $L_2$ norm is taken. Default `-1` (the trailing feature axis). |
| `eps` | `float` | — | `1e-12` | Lower clamp on the norm denominator. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. Tensor of the same shape as `x`, unit-$L_2$ along `axis` (except where the input norm is below `eps`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:l2_normalize -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
